# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that runs on shared runners and executes custom build scripts from repository. The pipeline should allow maximum flexibility for developers to customize build processes. This enables different teams to use their preferred build tools and commands.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - test
  - deploy

variables: # These are environment variables shared across all jobs in the pipeline, such as database urls or tokens which should not be hardcoded within job steps
    DB_URL: "https://database.com"

test_job:
  stage: test  
  before_script:
      - 'which ssh-agent || ( apt update && sudo apt install openssh-client )' # added to make sure we have the necessary client for key based authentication, also if not already installed in ubuntu image then it is being fetched. This line ensures that there exists an available one and can be used when needed
      - eval $(ssh-agent)  
  script:         
    - echo "Running tests..."         # Here you may want to run your own custom build process or commands, depending on the requirements of this project                        
       chmod +x ./scripts/test.sh ;./scripts/test.sh          
     
     services_job:  
        stage: deploy 
    before_script:         
         - echo "Initializing git..."              # This will run every time in order to pull the latest code from GitLab, only necessary if you're using a 'git over http/https', or have pushed new tags. You should remove this command unless there is an important thing that needs doing before pushing your repo
         - git fetch origin                       # Fetching all branches with their updates into our local environment  and keep it safe from error during update, we're expecting to pull the latest changes on every run of job in pipeline. The .git folder should be present at location where script is running by default    
       script:         
         - echo "Deploying app..."               # Here you may want deploy your application or command depending upon project requirements            
           chmod +x ./scripts/deploy.sh ;./scripts/deploy.sh
